lines.text: Penguin: Penguins are a group of flightless semi-aquatic sea birds which live almost exclusively in the Southern Hemisphere.
lines.subtitle_text: Penguin: Penguins are a group of flightless semi-aquatic sea birds which live almost exclusively in the Southern Hemisphere.

lines.text: Etymology: Only one species, the Galapagos penguin, lives at, and slightly north of, the equator.
lines.subtitle_text: Etymology: Only one species, the Galapagos penguin, lives at, and slightly north of, the equator.

lines.text: Systematics and evolution: Highly adapted for life in the ocean water, penguins have countershaded dark and white plumage and flippers for swimming.
lines.subtitle_text: Systematics and evolution: Highly adapted for life in the ocean water, penguins have countershaded dark and white plumage and flippers for swimming.

lines.text: Anatomy and physiology: Most penguins feed on krill, fish, squid and other forms of sea life which they catch with their bills and swallow whole while swimming.
lines.subtitle_text: Anatomy and physiology: Most penguins feed on krill, fish, squid and other forms of sea life which they catch with their bills and swallow whole while swimming.

lines.text: Penguins and humans: Only one species, the Galapagos penguin, lives at, and slightly north of, the equator.
lines.subtitle_text: Penguins and humans: Only one species, the Galapagos penguin, lives at, and slightly north of, the equator.

lines.text: Adélie penguin: Highly adapted for life in the ocean water, penguins have countershaded dark and white plumage and flippers for swimming.
lines.subtitle_text: Adélie penguin: Highly adapted for life in the ocean water, penguins have countershaded dark and white plumage and flippers for swimming.
